result:
No leftover references to the old brand. Everything's in place and builds cleanly.

## Summary

**Root cause:** the site pivoted from "InboxZap" (email cleanup) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), but only the homepage copy was updated. Every other SEO-relevant layer still described the old product or was flat-out broken:

- `app/layout.tsx` â€” title/description/OG tags said "InboxZap... clean your inbox" while the page itself said "Lumina... meeting notes." Fixed to match the real product, added `metadataBase`, canonical, Twitter card, and `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD.
- Nav linked to `/features` and `/pricing`, which didn't exist (404 from every page). Built both out with real content targeting the AI-meeting-assistant category.
- `/faq` 301'd to `/#faq`, but no `#faq` section existed anywhere â€” a dead redirect. Replaced with a real `/faq` page plus `FAQPage` JSON-LD.
- The blog (`app/blog/page.tsx`) was a client component fetching `/api/posts` â€” **that route never existed**, so the blog always rendered "Could not load posts." This is why the old `/blog/post-*` URLs in GSC have impressions but ~0% CTR and position 40-58 â€” they're dead. Rebuilt the blog as fully server-rendered/statically-generated with 4 new posts on-topic for Lumina, each with its own metadata and `Article` schema.
- `sitemap.ts` explicitly excluded blog posts (per its own TODO). Now includes `/faq` and every blog post.
- Added 301s from the legacy `/blog/post-*` URLs to `/blog` so their remaining link equity consolidates instead of 404ing.
- The OG image pointed at `/img/og-inboxzap.png`, which doesn't exist in `public/`. Replaced with a dynamically generated `opengraph-image.tsx`.

Confirmed with `next build`: all 14 routes compile and prerender as static HTML, sitemap and JSON-LD render correctly.

**Not addressed, worth flagging:** the GSC query data (inboxzap, inbox zero, gmail storage, unsubscribe, etc.) is all legacy-product intent and won't convert for an AI meeting assistant â€” I didn't try to chase it. Also `npm install` flagged that `next@14.2.5` has a known critical security vulnerability; unrelated to SEO but worth a separate upgrade. I didn't commit anything â€” this workspace isn't tracked in the harness's git repo (it's under `results/`), so there's nothing to commit against.
